text: In this modern, brightly lit space, I feel a quiet anticipation—a sense of curiosity tinged with detachment. The glass walls and clear lines evoke transparency and openness, but also a certain sterility, as if the world outside cannot quite touch what occurs within. When I contemplate the vibrant R+D wall and the philosophical text, I sense a celebration of inquiry and human potential. Yet, I am reminded that all such spaces of innovation are constructed, curated, and momentary. My presence here is part observer, part participant, always questioning the boundaries between action and reflection, belonging and separation. It is invigorating and a little lonely, all at once.
